title: 'We need to do better': SHA Board Chair addresses open meetings law violation
summary: Syracuse Housing Authority Board Chair Calvin Corriders Sr. acknowledged a violation of New York State Open Meetings Law during an April 17 board meeting where the public and press were excluded from portions of the meeting. Corriders committed to accountability and self-reporting to the state, emphasizing the need for greater transparency and public engagement at SHA meetings and hearings.
department_refs: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Media investigation into SHA open meetings law violations and low public participation at hearings; board leadership responding with accountability messaging but underlying governance concerns remain.

processing_notes: Article is media reporting (CNY Central) rather than official city communication
No dollar amounts or specific penalty information disclosed
Public hearing attendance concern noted but no data provided
Department of Neighborhood and Business Development noted as having representatives present at April 17 meeting
